title: Global Agent Auto-Resume Protocol
scope: Global — TQP queue atoms, isolated subagent sessions, and main-session long-running tasks when interrupted by timeout, crash, node disconnect, or model failure (e.g. 429 rate-limit, gateway restart).

files_inspected.purpose: TQP queue manager: claims queued/dispatched tasks, re-queues timed-out claims, hands non-CREST atoms to tqp-executor.sh.
Executor for non-CREST TQP atoms: claims dispatched rows, spawns exec-atom children via state_task_queue INSERT.
CREST sub-ticket dispatcher: handles Level 1/2 CREST dispatches and escalations.
Heartbeat watchdog: detects stalled/pending tasks, JSON↔PG divergence.
Detects cron failures, consecutive errors, stale processes.
Safe subagent dispatch helper; blocks cross-workspace mutating dispatches unless read-only or cwd override.
JSON mirror of task queue (audit trail only per L-075).

indexes.name: state_task_queue_pkey
indexes.def: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX state_task_queue_pkey ON public.state_task_queue USING btree (id)

indexes.name: idx_task_queue_tid
indexes.def: CREATE INDEX idx_task_queue_tid ON public.state_task_queue USING btree (tenant_id)

pg_schema.note: No composite index on (status, claimedby, parent_task_id). No status enum constraint. No previous_status trigger.

gaps_identified.issue: Claim timeout is hard-coded 30 min (1800s). A long-running atom (e.g. full backup, model-heavy CREST atom) can legitimately exceed 30 min and be incorrectly re-queued while still running.
gaps_identified.impact: False-positive re-queue leading to duplicate execution or claim collisions.

gaps_identified.issue: When no active session is found for a dispatched task, the processor re-queues it blindly without capturing why the session died. There is no checkpoint/last-completed-step concept.
gaps_identified.impact: Resumed atoms restart from scratch instead of continuing from last known good step.

gaps_identified.issue: Non-CREST atoms are handed to tqp-executor.sh but the parent task stays status='dispatched'. If the exec-atom child fails, the parent is never marked failed/resumable.
gaps_identified.impact: L-096 class silence: TQP claims atoms but has no failure-to-resume feedback loop.

gaps_identified.issue: Exec-atom child is created with status='queued' but the original atom transitions to status='running'. There is no mechanism to copy the child's final status back to the original atom or to resume a killed exec-atom.
gaps_identified.impact: Failure of the spawned session leaves the original atom stuck in 'running' or an orphan exec-atom in the queue.

gaps_identified.issue: CREST sub-ticket escalation goes to Yoda but there is no automatic retry/escalate-to-resume path for transient failures (429, gateway restart).
gaps_identified.impact: Rate-limited or interrupted CREST atoms wait for human intervention.

gaps_identified.issue: Detects cron failures but only writes a state file. No automatic resume/retry integration with the queue.
gaps_identified.impact: Cron jobs that fail due to transient model errors remain failed until next scheduled run or manual fix.

gaps_identified.issue: Cross-workspace mutating dispatch to agents outside the parent workspace is blocked by default. Forge (infra workspace) cannot be safely dispatched to modify main workspace scripts without Ken approval.
gaps_identified.impact: Build atoms for TKT-0319 must either run in main session (Yoda) or require explicit per-atom Ken approval.

gaps_identified.issue: state_task_queue lacks status and (status, claimedby, updated_at_ts) indexes. As queue grows, resume scans will slow.
gaps_identified.impact: Resume detector and re-queuer will degrade performance.

last_completed_step.description: Index of last fully completed step

context.description: Opaque JSON snapshot needed to resume (e.g. file paths, state keys, partial results)

recommended_implementation_atoms.title: Add resume columns/indexes and checkpoint table
recommended_implementation_atoms.brief: Add status/claimedby/updated_at_ts composite index to state_task_queue. Add resume_attempts, last_checkpoint JSONB columns. Create state_resume_checkpoints table (id, task_id, checkpoint_at, payload_hash).
recommended_implementation_atoms.risk: PG schema migration; low risk with idempotent CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS and ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS.

recommended_implementation_atoms.title: Build resume detector / watchdog
recommended_implementation_atoms.brief: Extend task-watchdog.sh and cron-health-check.sh to mark interrupted atoms as 'resumable' with failure_reason. Detect death by: cron lastStatus=error with retryable reason, session absence for dispatched tasks, claim timeout > computed adaptive timeout, gateway restart events.
recommended_implementation_atoms.risk: False positives if detector is too aggressive. Needs adjustable thresholds and Ken-approval for auto-retry of critical atoms.

recommended_implementation_atoms.title: Implement resume executor for TQP atoms
recommended_implementation_atoms.brief: Modify tqp-executor.sh and task-queue-processor.sh to: read last checkpoint, restore context, skip completed steps, resume exec-atom child. Update original atom status from child exec-atom status.
recommended_implementation_atoms.risk: Touches TQP hot path; must preserve idempotency gate and lock behaviour.

recommended_implementation_atoms.title: Add main-session/subagent resume path
recommended_implementation_atoms.brief: For non-TQP subagent/main-session tasks, add a lightweight resume registry (state/resume-registry.json or PG) that tracks long-running tasks. On session death, the heartbeat re-spawns using sessions_spawn with previous context.
recommended_implementation_atoms.risk: Needs HITL gate for any destructive main-session recovery.

recommended_implementation_atoms.title: End-to-end simulation
recommended_implementation_atoms.brief: Create synthetic long-running atoms in TQP and main session, kill them mid-flight, verify auto-resume completes and output matches uninterrupted run.
recommended_implementation_atoms.risk: Simulation must be isolated from production tickets and crons.

out_of_scope_for_tkt0319: True distributed HA/failover across OC2 nodes (P1 infra).
Replaying/correcting already-lost in-flight LLM responses (we resume from checkpoint, not replay model tokens).
Auto-resume of intentionally paused waiting-for-Ken atoms.
risks: Cross-workspace dispatch policy (subagent-dispatch.sh) requires explicit Ken approval for Forge to modify main workspace scripts.
Resume without idempotency could duplicate side effects. Every resume-capable atom must expose idempotency tokens.
Aggressive death detection may create resume storms during model outages or gateway restarts. Need backoff and global rate-limit.
next_step: Review with Ken, approve Atom 2 implementation, and dispatch schema/index migration to Forge (requires workspace-mutation approval).
